name: Embalse de la Serena and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer — circular from la Puebla de Alcocer
name_original: Embalse de la Serena y Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer — circular desde la Puebla de Alcocer
region_intro: Touring cycling around Puebla De Alcocer features routes primarily centered around large reservoirs and historical landmarks. The landscape is characterized by expansive water bodies like the Orellana and La Serena reservoirs, interspersed with rolling terrain and the occasional ascent to historical castles. The region offers a mix of natural trails and paths connecting local villages and points of interest. Best touring cycling routes around Puebla De Alcocer The most popular touring cycling route is Guadiana Natural Trail: Orellana Reservoir Loop, a 27.0 miles (43.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete. This route follows a natural trail around the Orellana Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Embalse de la Serena and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer — circular from la Puebla de Alcocer, a difficult 45.4 miles (73.1 km) path. This route explores the area around La Serena reservoir and includes the castle of Puebla de Alcocer. Local touring cyclists also love the Garcia de Sola Reservoir and Herrera del Duque Castle Loop, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail leading through reservoir landscapes and past historical castles, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Puebla De Alcocer is defined by large reservoirs, historical castles, and varied terrain with moderate to difficult climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, including several moderate and difficult routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 39 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Puebla De Alcocer's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Puebla De Alcocer?
A: There are 10 touring cycling routes available around Puebla De Alcocer, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes range from moderate to difficult, with no easy routes currently listed.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for touring cycling in Puebla De Alcocer?
A: The terrain around Puebla De Alcocer is characterized by expansive water bodies like the Orellana and La Serena reservoirs, rolling hills, and occasional ascents to historical castles. You'll find a mix of natural trails and paths connecting local villages.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Puebla De Alcocer?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021409446/embalse-de-la-serena-and-castillo-de-puebla-de-alcocer-circular-from-la-puebla-de-alcocer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Serena and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer — circular from la Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 73.1 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021420835/garcia-de-sola-reservoir-and-herrera-del-duque-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garcia de Sola Reservoir and Herrera del Duque Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 60.7 km.

Q: What kind of historical landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: You can explore several historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5460742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021409446/embalse-de-la-serena-and-castillo-de-puebla-de-alcocer-circular-from-la-puebla-de-alcocer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Serena and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer — circular from la Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; route passes by it. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021420835/garcia-de-sola-reservoir-and-herrera-del-duque-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garcia de Sola Reservoir and Herrera del Duque Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; also takes you past a historical castle.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for beginners or families with children?
A: The touring cycling routes around Puebla De Alcocer are generally moderate to difficult, with no easy routes listed. This suggests that most trails might be more suitable for experienced cyclists or families with older, more capable riders. The shortest moderate route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa hormigonada de Peloche loop from Dehesa Boyal&lt;/a&gt; at 20.1 km.

Q: What natural attractions can I discover while cycling in the region?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, primarily centered around its large reservoirs. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garcia de Sola Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serena Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the Orellana Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt;, a distinctive summit, is also a notable natural landmark.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Puebla De Alcocer?
A: Given the region's climate, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the most pleasant times for touring cycling in Puebla De Alcocer. Temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there any wild swimming spots near the cycling routes?
A: The region is known for its large reservoirs, such as the Orellana and La Serena. While specific wild swimming spots aren't explicitly listed for these routes, these reservoirs often have designated areas or accessible shores where swimming might be possible. Always check local regulations and safety information before entering the water.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Puebla De Alcocer?
A: The komoot community has used these routes over 39 times, indicating a good level of interest. Cyclists often praise the scenic routes around the large reservoirs and the opportunity to combine cycling with exploring historical castles. The varied terrain, offering moderate to difficult climbs, is also appreciated by those seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any routes that follow natural trails?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021414907/guadiana-natural-trail-orellana-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail: Orellana Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that follows a natural trail around the Orellana Reservoir, offering a scenic experience away from paved roads.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available?
A: The longest touring cycling route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021409446/embalse-de-la-serena-and-castillo-de-puebla-de-alcocer-circular-from-la-puebla-de-alcocer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Serena and Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer — circular from la Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 73.1 km and is classified as difficult.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by religious buildings?
A: While not directly on the listed routes, the region around Puebla De Alcocer features historical religious sites. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5673577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro Apóstol in Casas de Don Pedro&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6204238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Remedies&lt;/a&gt; nearby, which could be explored as detours or part of a custom route.
tips:
Cerro Masatrigo is a hill located in the municipality of Esparragosa de Lares. This hill is known for its almost perfect conical shape, which has earned it the nickname "the magic mountain."
It rises 501 meters above sea level and 161 meters above the surrounding terrain. After the construction of the La Serena Dam, Cerro Masatrigo became a peninsula surrounded by the waters of the reservoir.

The La Serena Dam, located in the province of Badajoz (Extremadura), is one of the most impressive hydraulic infrastructures in Europe.
Location: Zújar River, in the region of La Serena
It is part of the La Siberia Biosphere Reserve.
It houses inland islands such as Cerro Masatrigo, considered a "magic mountain" for its perfect conical shape.
A SPA (Special Protection Area for Birds), ideal for watching aquatic birds and birds of prey.

The castle of Puebla de Alcocer is an imposing castle built during the 12th and 13th centuries, remodeled in the 15th century by Don Gutierre de Sotomayor, Master of the Order of Alcántara, thanks to the granting of a license from the King of Castile, Juan II.

It is located on an elongated rocky mass, at the highest point of Puebla de Alcocer, from where the movements of the adversaries were monitored for kilometers around. Today the regions of La Serena and La Siberia are contemplated and the fortification can be seen from several points.
